title: THE FEASIBILITY OF UTILIZING OIL SHALE
author: N.F. Rakhmatova, A.A. Shokhakimova, N.T. Rakhmatullaeva, D.K. Abdullaeva , U.N. Rakhmatov
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Oil shale is an alternative fossil fuel resource that has the potential to reduce dependence on conventional crude oil. This paper explores the feasibility of utilizing oil shale as an energy source, considering its availability, extraction methods, economic viability, and environmental impact. While oil shale presents an opportunity for energy diversification, challenges such as high extraction costs and environmental concerns must be addressed.
steps_raw: Oil shale is an alternative fossil fuel resource that has the potential to reduce dependence on conventional crude oil. This paper explores the feasibility of utilizing oil shale as an energy source, considering its availability, extraction methods, economic viability, and environmental impact. While oil shale presents an opportunity for energy diversification, challenges such as high extraction costs and environmental concerns must be addressed.
keywords: oil shale, shale oil, fossil fuel, kerogen, energy resource, extraction methods, surface retorting, in-situ conversion, economic feasibility, environmental impact, CO2 emissions, water consumption, land degradation, waste management, renewable energy alternative, oil reserves, production cost, sustainable energy, energy diversification
